[**Notodden**](//upload.wikimedia.org/wikipedia/commons/transcoded/e/e0/Notodden.ogg/Notodden.ogg.mp3 "Play audio")[ⓘ](/wiki/File:Notodden.ogg "File:Notodden.ogg") is a [town](/wiki/List_of_towns_and_cities_in_Norway "List of towns and cities in Norway") in [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality") in [Telemark](/wiki/Telemark "Telemark") county, [Norway](/wiki/Norway "Norway"). It is also the [administrative centre](/wiki/Administrative_centre "Administrative centre") of the municipality. The town is located at the north end of the lake [Heddalsvatnet](/wiki/Heddalsvatnet "Heddalsvatnet"), at the mouth of the river [Tinnelva](/wiki/Tinnelva "Tinnelva"). The villages of [Heddal](/wiki/Heddal_(village) "Heddal (village)") and [Yli](/wiki/Yli "Yli") lie about 5 kilometres (3.1 mi) to the west of the town. The villages of [Hjuksebø](/wiki/Hjukseb%C3%B8 "Hjuksebø") and [Hjuksevelta](/wiki/Hjuksevelta "Hjuksevelta") both lie about 8 kilometres (5.0 mi) to the south of the town. The [town of Kongsberg](/wiki/Kongsberg_(town) "Kongsberg (town)") is located about 28 kilometres (17 mi) to the east of the town. The village of [Bolkesjø](/wiki/Bolkesj%C3%B8 "Bolkesjø") is located about 20 kilometres (12 mi) to the north of the town.[[4]](#cite_note-snl-4)

The 7.32-square-kilometre (1,810-acre) town has a population (2022) of 9,041 and a [population density](/wiki/Population_density "Population density") of 1,235 inhabitants per square kilometre (3,200/sq mi).[[2]](#cite_note-ssb-2)

The [Notodden Church](/wiki/Notodden_Church "Notodden Church") is located in the town centre. The [European route E134](/wiki/European_route_E134 "European route E134") highway passes through the town from east to west.

The town's old industrial base is located near the lake shore. This is where [Norsk Hydro](/wiki/Norsk_Hydro "Norsk Hydro")'s facilities were built.[[4]](#cite_note-snl-4)

## History

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden_(town)&action=edit&section=1 "Edit section: History")]

The village of Notodden was established as a [kjøpstad](/wiki/Kj%C3%B8pstad "Kjøpstad") on 1 January 1913. This essentially granted [town status](/wiki/List_of_towns_and_cities_in_Norway "List of towns and cities in Norway") and special economic rights for the growing urban area. Under the law, this meant that the village was removed from [Heddal Municipality](/wiki/Heddal "Heddal") and the new town was given self-governing authority, essentially making it a municipality of its own. Initially, the new town had a population of 4,821 people.[[5]](#cite_note-Dag-5)

During the 1960s, there were many municipal mergers across Norway due to the work of the [Schei Committee](/wiki/Schei_Committee "Schei Committee"). On 1 January 1964, the town (population: 7,523) was merged with the following areas to create the new [Notodden Municipality](/wiki/Notodden_Municipality "Notodden Municipality"):[[5]](#cite_note-Dag-5)

* most of the municipality of [Gransherad](/wiki/Gransherad "Gransherad") (population: 1,115) - except for the upper Jondalen valley which became part of [Kongsberg Municipality](/wiki/Kongsberg_Municipality "Kongsberg Municipality")
* the [Rudsgrendi](/wiki/Rudsgrendi "Rudsgrendi") area of [Hovin Municipality](/wiki/Hovin,_Telemark "Hovin, Telemark") (population: 21)
* the entire municipality of [Heddal](/wiki/Heddal "Heddal") (population: 4,844)

## Climate

[[edit](/w/index.php?title=Notodden_(town)&action=edit&section=3 "Edit section: Climate")]

Notodden has a [humid continental climate](/wiki/Humid_continental_climate "Humid continental climate") (Dfb). Situated inland at low altitude in the Telemark region, is one of the warmer towns in Norway in summer, but winters can be cold. The weather station at [Notodden Airport](/wiki/Notodden_Airport "Notodden Airport") has been recording since March 1970 (temperature and wind speed). Precipitation data is from a different station in Notodden. The all-time high temperature 33.3 °C (91.9 °F) was recorded on 27 July 2018.
